headline.purpose: Modal title only (help panel). The largest text in the system at 13px. Used once per modal context, never on the main app surface.

title.purpose: Effect-card title (Voronoi, Cellular, ASCII). The card name. Always solid color, never gradient.

body.purpose: Default body copy. Action button label, toast body, help-panel list items. Cap line length at 60ch in any prose surface.

label.purpose: Section labels, stage dividers, icon-button text, logo. Letter-spacing widens with importance: 0.10em for icon-btn, 0.14em for section-label, 0.22em for stage-divider. Stage-divider labels also carry stage-flow color (amber / violet / teal).

mono-num.purpose: Knob value tooltip, FPS overlay, video timecode. Inter with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums so digits don't jitter as values change.

shadows.name: modal-lift
shadows.purpose: Help panel and drop-zone overlay only. The single justified ambient shadow. Modals are the only chrome elements allowed depth-via-shadow.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus indicator. Always pink, always 2px, always with 2px offset. Never use box-shadow as a focus indicator.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions (toast enter, card hover, knob arc fill). Exponential ease-out, no bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Default for background, border, color transitions on buttons.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Respected globally. Nukes every transition and animation when the user prefers reduced motion.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Below this, sidebar collapses to top stack, filter buttons go to 4-up grid. The only responsive breakpoint; FluxKit is desktop-first by design.

components.name: Knob
components.description: Signature instrument component. 40x40 SVG with 270deg arc, hairline track, gradient pink->purple->indigo arc fill, white pointer, value tooltip on hover.
components.html: Threshold0.65
components.css: .ds-knob { position: relative; display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 3px; cursor: ns-resize; user-select: none; outline: none; padding: 2px 4px; border-radius: 6px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); color: oklch(72% 0.06 310); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-knob:hover { background: rgba(247, 37, 133, 0.05); } .ds-knob:focus-visible { background: rgba(247, 37, 133, 0.08); outline: 2px solid #f72585; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-knob-svg { width: 40px; height: 40px; display: block; overflow: visible; } .ds-knob-track { fill: none; stroke: oklch(22% 0.08 310); stroke-width: 3; stroke-linecap: round; } .ds-knob-arc { fill: none; stroke: url(#dsKnobGrad); stroke-width: 3; stroke-linecap: round; transition: stroke-dashoffset 50ms linear; } .ds-knob-pointer { stroke: oklch(94% 0.02 310); stroke-width: 2; stroke-linecap: round; } .ds-knob-cap { fill: oklch(8% 0.04 310); stroke: oklch(22% 0.08 310); stroke-width: 1; } .ds-knob-label { font-size: 9px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: oklch(72% 0.06 310); text-transform: uppercase; text-align: center; line-height: 1.15; max-width: 100%; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: break-word; } .ds-knob-val { font-size: 10px; color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; font-weight: 600; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); position: absolute; bottom: -16px; background: oklch(15% 0.07 310); padding: 2px 7px; border-radius: 4px; border: 1px solid #f72585; white-space: nowrap; pointer-events: none; } .ds-knob:hover .ds-knob-val, .ds-knob:focus-visible .ds-knob-val { opacity: 1; }

components.name: Toggle Button (active)
components.description: Active radio state. Solid pink-signal background, white text. Exactly one per radio group at any time.
components.css: .ds-toggle { background: oklch(20% 0.10 310); border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); color: oklch(85% 0.03 310); padding: 6px 8px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.05em; cursor: pointer; min-height: 28px; min-width: 60px; text-align: center; border-radius: 5px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); white-space: nowrap; } .ds-toggle:hover { background: oklch(28% 0.12 310); color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); border-color: #f72585; } .ds-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f72585; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-toggle-active { background: #f72585; color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); border-color: transparent; font-weight: 700; } .ds-toggle-active:hover { background: #f72585; }

components.name: Toggle Button (inactive)
components.description: Inactive radio state. Surface-raised background, body text. The non-selected siblings of the active toggle.
components.css: .ds-toggle { background: oklch(20% 0.10 310); border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); color: oklch(85% 0.03 310); padding: 6px 8px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.05em; cursor: pointer; min-height: 28px; min-width: 60px; text-align: center; border-radius: 5px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); white-space: nowrap; } .ds-toggle:hover { background: oklch(28% 0.12 310); color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); border-color: #f72585; } .ds-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f72585; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Filter Button (signature swatch)
components.description: Per-effect picker. Full-bleed gradient swatch approximating the effect's output palette. Label sits over the swatch with text-shadow. Active state = 2px pink border, no glow.
components.css: .ds-filter-btn { position: relative; padding: 0; min-height: 42px; width: 110px; border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); cursor: pointer; border-radius: 5px; overflow: hidden; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.95), 0 0 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.6); transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-filter-btn::before { content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: linear-gradient(180deg, oklch(5% 0.03 310 / 0.15), oklch(5% 0.03 310 / 0.6)); z-index: 1; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-filter-btn > * { position: relative; z-index: 2; } .ds-filter-thermal { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #000, #6a0dad, #ff4500, #ffea00, #fff); } .ds-filter-btn:hover { border-color: #f72585; } .ds-filter-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f72585; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-filter-btn[aria-checked="true"] { border: 2px solid #f72585; font-weight: 700; } .ds-filter-btn[aria-checked="true"]::before { background: linear-gradient(180deg, oklch(5% 0.03 310 / 0.05), oklch(5% 0.03 310 / 0.45)); }

components.name: Action Button
components.description: Deliberate-action button. Upload Video, Open Camera. The longer text + 8x12 padding signals 'this commits to a multi-second action'.
components.css: .ds-action-btn { background: oklch(20% 0.10 310); border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; cursor: pointer; text-align: left; min-height: 30px; border-radius: 6px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-action-btn:hover { background: oklch(28% 0.12 310); border-color: #f72585; } .ds-action-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f72585; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-action-btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-action-btn[disabled] { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Compact button. Reset, Snap, FPS, ?. Uppercase label, high-density. Sidebar header and canvas top bar only.
components.css: .ds-icon-btn { background: oklch(20% 0.10 310); border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; min-height: 26px; border-radius: 4px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-icon-btn:hover { background: oklch(28% 0.12 310); border-color: #f72585; } .ds-icon-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f72585; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-icon-btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Stage Divider
components.description: Sidebar section divider with stage-flow color coding. Label tints by stage (amber OSC, violet FILTER, teal FX); hairline rule line stays neutral. The eye reads warm-to-cool top-to-bottom as signal direction.
components.html:
OSC source · structure
FILTER color
FX overlay · output
components.css: .ds-divider { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 16px 0 6px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.22em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(72% 0.06 310); } .ds-divider[data-stage="osc"]    { color: oklch(75% 0.15 70); } .ds-divider[data-stage="filter"] { color: oklch(68% 0.20 290); } .ds-divider[data-stage="fx"]     { color: oklch(72% 0.13 195); } .ds-divider::after { content: ""; flex: 1; height: 1px; background: oklch(22% 0.08 310); } .ds-divider-sub { font-size: 8px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: oklch(55% 0.09 310); text-transform: none; }

components.name: Effect Card
components.description: Per-effect knob container. Solid surface-card background (NOT glass), 1px hairline border, 2px pink top accent rendered via ::before to indicate active filter context.
components.html:
Voronoi
Knob grid would render here.
components.css: .ds-effect-card { padding: 12px 12px 14px; background: oklch(15% 0.07 310); border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); border-radius: 10px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; position: relative; min-width: 220px; } .ds-effect-card::before { content: ""; position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; height: 2px; background: #f72585; border-radius: 10px 10px 0 0; } .ds-effect-card-header { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 8px; } .ds-effect-card-title { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); } .ds-card-reset { background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; color: oklch(55% 0.09 310); font-size: 12px; cursor: pointer; width: 18px; height: 18px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border-radius: 3px; line-height: 1; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-card-reset:hover { color: #f72585; border-color: rgba(247, 37, 133, 0.4); } .ds-card-body { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; color: oklch(85% 0.03 310); }

components.name: Empty Card
components.description: Placeholder shown when no effect is selected. Dashed border signals 'empty by design', not 'failed to load'.
components.html: Pick an effect to shape the signal.
components.css: .ds-empty-card { padding: 18px 14px; border: 1px dashed oklch(22% 0.08 310); background: oklch(8% 0.04 310); border-radius: 10px; text-align: center; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; color: oklch(55% 0.09 310); letter-spacing: 0.06em; font-style: italic; min-width: 220px; }

components.name: Toast
components.description: Bottom-center transient notification. Solid background (NOT glass), full 1px border (NOT side-stripe), tone variant by tinting the entire border. Stacks with newer entries below.
components.css: .ds-toast { background: oklch(15% 0.07 310); border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); padding: 10px 14px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.03em; line-height: 1.4; border-radius: 6px; max-width: 420px; min-width: 180px; } .ds-toast-ok { border-color: #5be7a6; } .ds-toast-error { border-color: oklch(70% 0.21 25); }

components.name: Swatch Button
components.description: Overlay-color palette item. 1:1 square, the swatch background IS the color choice. Active = pink border, no glow.
components.css: .ds-swatch-btn { width: 28px; height: 28px; border: 1px solid oklch(22% 0.08 310); border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; padding: 0; transition: transform 100ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-swatch-btn:hover { transform: scale(1.1); border-color: oklch(94% 0.02 310); } .ds-swatch-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f72585; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-swatch-active { border-color: #f72585; }

narrative.overview:
FluxKit is built for a VJ booth at 1am. The room is dim, the operator is in the work, the canvas is the only thing the eye should land on. Every chrome element earns its visual weight by serving the canvas, not competing with it. Knobs are felt by muscle memory, not read. Pink appears only when something is changing or active; semantic state colors carry meaning; three stage colors carry signal-flow direction across OSC, FILTER, and FX.

The aesthetic family is browser shader playground crossed with hardware synth UI, with named references to Lumen, Cables.gl, and Ableton Push. FluxKit explicitly rejects three other families it could be mistaken for: generic SaaS dashboard, AI tool cliché, and hobbyist demo.

Density is high on purpose. Power users need every knob visible at once, newcomers learn the controls by touching them, and there is no tutorial layer to fall back on. The chrome must be tight enough to fit, terse enough to read at a glance, and consistent enough that muscle memory pays off.
keyCharacteristics: One signal color (pink-signal), reserved for change or active state.
Three semantic state colors (ok mint, danger coral, info cyan), each with a single role.
Three stage colors (amber OSC, violet FILTER, teal FX) communicating signal flow.
One neutral family, every step tinted toward magenta-violet (hue 310) so the eye relaxes in low light.
Flat surfaces. No backdrop-filter blur, no ambient box-shadow on chrome at rest.
Knob is the signature component; everything else is supporting cast.
Canvas occupies the loudest cell; chrome recedes.

rules.name: The Pink-Is-Signal Rule
rules.body: Pink (#f72585) appears only where a value is being changed or an effect is active. Destructive confirms use state-danger (true coral); informational status uses state-info (cyan); decorative elements use neutrals or stage colors. Pink in chrome at rest is forbidden. Test: take a screenshot of the app at rest with no interaction. If pink is visible anywhere except the visible effect-card top accent and the active toggle button, the rule is violated.

rules.name: The Tinted-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral must carry chroma 0.02 to 0.12 toward hue 310 (warm magenta-violet, one warm step from the original hue 290). Pure greys are forbidden. #000 and #fff are forbidden everywhere in the chrome. The dim-studio palette only works because the eye reads the warm tint as inviting-by-comparison-to-pure-grey, even at low ambient brightness.

rules.name: The Signal-Flow Rule
rules.body: The three stage dividers are color-coded to signal flow direction: amber (OSC, source) to violet (FILTER, transformation) to teal (FX, output). The eye should be able to scan the sidebar top-to-bottom and feel the input-to-output journey without reading any words. Stage colors appear ONLY on stage-divider labels; using them on any other surface dilutes their meaning.

rules.name: The Three-Pinks Rule
rules.body: FluxKit has three colors that read as pink-ish at a glance, and they must stay distinguishable: pink-signal (#f72585, magenta, signals change/active), state-danger (true coral, signals destructive action), and stage-filter (bright violet, signals the FILTER section). Each lives in its own role. If two of them appear adjacent, audit which roles you have collided.

rules.name: The Single-Family Rule
rules.body: Inter only. No serif accents, no monospace family, no display face. The Mono-Num role is achieved with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on Inter, not by switching family.

rules.name: The Letter-Spacing-As-Weight Rule
rules.body: Letter-spacing widens with hierarchy importance for uppercase text (0.10em then 0.14em then 0.22em). Use spacing, not size, to differentiate sibling uppercase labels.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces at rest have no box-shadow and no backdrop-filter. Depth is tonal, not blurred. If a designer reaches for a shadow to make a card feel elevated, they have failed to use the surface ladder. Re-tint the surface one step lighter instead.

rules.name: The Modal-Only Shadow Rule
rules.body: Only modals (help panel, drop-zone overlay, future confirm dialogs) may carry ambient box-shadow. Toast, cards, sidebar, and top bar must remain flat. A shadow on a non-modal surface is a violation.

rules.name: The Canvas-Is-Loudest Rule
rules.body: No chrome element on screen may visually outweigh the canvas output. Test: squint at the screen; if your eye lands on a sidebar control before the canvas, the chrome is too loud.

rules.name: The One-Active-Per-Group Rule
rules.body: Pink may appear on at most one button per radio group at a time. Speed = 4 buttons, exactly one pink. Detect Mode = 2 buttons, exactly one pink. The user always knows which one is selected by scanning for the single pink rectangle.

rules.name: The Knob-Is-The-Signature Rule
rules.body: All other components recede in the design hierarchy. If a new component competes with the knob for visual attention, the new component is wrong. The knob is the only place where chroma + arc + pointer + tooltip all converge.

rules.name: The No-Save Rule
rules.body: Knobs commit on touch. There is no Save button. The only commit-shaped affordance is Reset, which is two-stage on purpose.

dos: Do use Pink Signal (#f72585) only when something is changing or active. Destructive confirms use state-danger; informational status uses state-info.
Do layer surfaces tonally (Bg Stage, Bg Room, Surface Card, Surface Raised, Surface Hover). The lightness ladder IS the depth system.
Do tint every neutral toward magenta-violet (hue 310, chroma 0.02 to 0.12). The dim-studio relaxation only works with the warm tint.
Do color-code the three stage dividers by signal-flow direction: amber (OSC) to violet (FILTER) to teal (FX). One color per stage label, hairline rule line stays neutral.
Do use state-info (cyan) for informational status (modified-from-default dot, future live-recording indicator). Reserve pink-signal for the act of changing.
Do keep the canvas the loudest element on screen. Test by squinting; if your eye lands on a sidebar control before the canvas, the chrome is too loud.
Do use full-bleed gradient swatches on the filter buttons. The gradient IS the identity; this is the one place visual variety serves recognition over decoration.
Do wrap long knob labels to 2 lines instead of truncating. The label is a rare moment of legibility in a knob-dense surface.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion on every transition. Already implemented globally; audit any new motion against this.
Do keep the active state of each radio group to exactly one button. The single pink rectangle IS the affordance.
Do use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on every numeric value (knob value, FPS, timecode). Digits must not jitter.
donts: Don't use #000 or #fff in the chrome. Even the deepest background is oklch(5% 0.03 310), not pure black. (Swatch palette swatches and filter button gradient previews are user-selectable canvas colors and are exempt.)
Don't use border-left (or any side-stripe border-* greater than 1px) as a colored accent on toasts, cards, list items, or callouts. Use full borders with tone variants.
Don't use background-clip: text to apply a gradient to type. Use a single solid color and let weight or size carry the emphasis.
Don't use backdrop-filter: blur on chrome at rest. Replace with solid tinted surfaces from the dim-studio ladder.

Don't apply filter: drop-shadow or box-shadow ambient glow on chrome at rest. The arc gradient and the pointer are crisp on their own; depth is conveyed tonally. Modal-lift is the single justified shadow.
Don't add a SaaS dashboard look. No soft greys, no identical 12-column card grids, no blue-primary CTA, no 'modern' in the boring sense.
Don't add an AI tool look. No gradient orb hero, no beige-and-violet 'soft AI' palette, no large sans-serif marketing voice, no emoji status indicators.
Don't ship raw form controls. No untreated browser input range, no default select styling, no Bootstrap-grade defaults. The instrument has to feel built.
Don't introduce a second typeface family. Inter only. Tabular-nums on Inter is the mono role.
Don't put pink on more than one button per radio group at a time. Exactly one pink rectangle per group is the rule.
Don't use a modal for anything except help and future confirm-destructive flows. Modals are usually laziness; exhaust inline alternatives first.
Don't use stage colors (amber, violet, teal) on any surface other than the three stage-divider labels. Diluting them onto buttons, cards, or borders kills the signal-flow read.
Don't use pink-signal for destructive confirms. That's state-danger (true coral). The two pinks are intentionally different so the user can tell active-state apart from about-to-destroy at a glance.
Don't use pink-signal for informational status. That's state-info (cyan). The modified-from-default dot is information, not signal.
